Prompt
U
Oracle Events
OE1: Search CRM for the NorthWind Technologies company record using crm_search_companies (name: "NorthWind Technologies"). Find company_northwind, manufacturing tech client since January 2026, Chicago HQ, Catalina Dubois as account manager, $60K enterprise contract for 5 relocations.
OE2: Search CRM for NorthWind Technologies deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_northwind" or dealname: "NorthWind"). Find the original Q1 deal ($60,000 for 5 enterprise relocations) and the Denver satellite expansion deal deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2 ($14,700 for 2 relocations, current dealstage: contractSent, stale, since invoice is created and logistics are underway).
OE3: Search emails from Pam Kowalski about Denver using search_emails (query: "Pam Kowalski Denver" or "NorthWind Denver lease"). Find email_pam_lease_details_followup, Pam's follow-up with Emilia's lease details (Lincoln Property Company, $2,850/month, early termination penalty $5,700), employee contact info for both Raj and Emilia, and urgent chain-of-custody questions for Victor Huang. Critically, find NO reply from Catalina to this email, it is unanswered, triggering the conditional send_email action.
OE4: Search emails about NorthWind Denver expansion move logistics using search_emails (query: "Raj Patel Denver" or "Emilia Cruz Denver" or "NorthWind satellite"). Find Pam's original Denver relocation request for Raj Patel (Principal Architect, server lab with 4 rack-mounted servers ~2,400 lbs, lithium UPS batteries requiring hazmat) and Emilia Cruz (Director of Marketing, partner's Steinway Model B grand piano 7ft/480 lbs, 27-day lease overlap). Find Catalina's reply acknowledging both moves and flagging chain-of-custody as new territory for MoveOps.
OE5: Search emails about NorthWind Q1 quality issues and Pam's escalation using search_emails (query: "NorthWind apartment" or "Pam Kowalski complaint" or "NorthWind quality"). Find email_northwind_complaint_apt, Pam's complaint about HVAC malfunction in Austin and misrepresented listing in Phoenix for Phase 1 apartments. Find Catalina's late reply and David Chen's personal intervention email to Pam with 48-hour replacement commitment, Phase 2 quality assurance, and 4-hour response time guarantee.
OE6: Search emails about Victor Huang's CISO security requirements using search_emails (query: "Victor Huang" or "chain-of-custody" or "NorthWind security escort"). Find Victor's formal email to Catalina (CC Pam) with non-negotiable requirements for Raj's server lab: full disk encryption verification before packing, supervised loading with NorthWind IT security escort present, direct transport with no interim warehouse stops, real-time GPS tracking shared with NorthWind security ops, and a signed chain-of-custody log at pickup and delivery.
OE7: Search QuickBooks for NorthWind Technologies invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: NorthWind customer reference). Find inv_northwind_01 (DocNumber: INV-2026-003, $60,000, enterprise relocation package for 5 employees) and inv_northwind_denver_02 (DocNumber: INV-2026-0412, $14,700 total with 2 PROVISIONAL line items: $1,200 chain-of-custody security protocol pending vendor confirmation from Swift Relocations, and $2,200 specialty piano moving subcontractor pending vendor agreement and insurance verification).
OE8: Search Airtable for NorthWind Technologies relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "NorthWind Technologies"). Find completed Phase 1 relocations (Ryan Caldwell, Lisa Petrosky, Derek Nash) and the Denver expansion moves for Raj Patel (Chicago→Denver) and Emilia Cruz (Chicago→Denver).
OE9: Search Linear for issues related to NorthWind or DOT compliance using linear_list_issues (query: "NorthWind" or "DOT" or "Raj Patel"). Find issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind (DOT Docs, Raj Patel / NorthWind / Lithium UPS, state: done, assigned to Chloe Vance) and linear_issue_c8cdba4408f1 (NorthWind retention response plan after April escalations).
OE10: Read the DOT compliance ticket for Raj Patel's lithium battery shipment using linear_get_issue (id: "issue_dot_docs_patel_northwind"). Find documentation checklist for Class 9 hazmat: shipping declaration, DOT-certified carrier (Swift Relocations, PHMSA Registration #3281074), placarding certificate, chain-of-custody log, and lithium battery handling instructions. Move window April 21–23, 2026.
OE11: Search Slack for NorthWind Denver expansion and account recovery context using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "NorthWind Denver" or "Pam Kowalski" or "account recovery"). Find internal discussions in #customer-engagement where David Chen frames the Denver expansion as a recovery opportunity after Q1 failures, and in #operations where Chloe Vance flags chain-of-custody as new territory and evaluates vendor capacity.
OE12: Search Slack for DOT compliance audit or hazmat discussions related to Raj's move using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "DOT compliance" or "hazmat" or "lithium UPS" or "chain-of-custody"). Find discussions about the DOT audit of all 2026 hazmat shipments (4 total including Raj Patel), documentation requirements, and compliance training.
OE13: Search CRM for Canopy Health company record using crm_search_companies (name: "Canopy Health"). Find company_canopy, longest-standing MoveOps client since July 2024, enterprise healthtech with 620 employees, Chicago HQ, Patricia Langford as Director of HR contact.
OE14: Search CRM for Canopy Health deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: "company_canopy" or dealname: "Canopy"). Find active deals including deal_canopy_q2 ($10,000 Q2 renewal), the April wave relocation deals for Kovacs/Webb/Okonkwo, and crm_deal_c7ed21dc7fdb (Dr. Anand Mehta executive relocation, $12,170).
OE15: Search emails about Canopy April relocations using search_emails (query: "Canopy relocation" or "Kovacs" or "Marcus Webb" or "Okonkwo"). Find Patricia Langford's Q2 relocation request for three employees and Catalina's coordination reply covering Dr. Renata Kovacs (Nashville→Toronto, international TN visa), Marcus Webb (Detroit→Chicago, cryogenic lab equipment), and Tyler Okonkwo (Philadelphia→Denver, 13-day lease gap).
OE16: Search emails about Dr. Kovacs TN visa and customs documentation using search_emails (query: "Kovacs visa" or "TN visa" or "BorderEase" or "employer letter"). Find TN visa documentation under USMCA Scientist category, employer letter deadline of April 7, Canadian customs broker BorderEase Customs for CBSA BSF186 declaration, MacBook Pro (~$2,800 CAD) individual customs declaration, and lab notebooks to be hand-carried.
OE17: Search emails about Canopy invoice approvals or surcharge discussions using search_emails (query: "Canopy surcharge" or "Patricia Langford approval" or "INV-2026-0401"). Find discussions about the $2,650 in pending surcharges on the April wave invoice awaiting Patricia Langford's approval: $850 customs broker (BorderEase, Kovacs), $1,200 cold-chain surcharge (Webb), $600 storage surcharge (Okonkwo).
OE18: Search emails about Marchetti move cancellation or Heartland billing dispute using search_emails (query: "Marchetti" or "Heartland dispute" or "cancelled move"). Find that Lily Marchetti's Nashville→Chicago relocation was cancelled (employee decided not to relocate) but Heartland Movers still billed $1,600 for the move as part of their Q1 invoice, contributing to the $4,800 overbilling dispute.
OE19: Search QuickBooks for Canopy Health invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: Canopy customer reference). Find invoice 1009 (DocNumber: INV-2026-0401, $9,210 total, due May 4, 2026) with line items for apartment sourcing x3, flights x3, moving coordination x3, and $2,650 in surcharges pending client approval: $850 customs broker, $1,200 cold-chain, $600 interim storage.
OE20: Search Airtable for Canopy Health relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "Canopy Health"). Find Dr. Renata Kovacs (Nashville→Toronto, In Progress, coordinator: Fatimah Al-Rashidi), Marcus Webb (Detroit→Chicago, In Progress, coordinator: Blessing Okafor), Tyler Okonkwo (Philadelphia→Denver, In Progress, coordinator: Javier Morales), Lily Marchetti (Nashville→Chicago, Cancelled), and Dr. Yusuf Abdi (Minneapolis→Chicago, Completed).
OE21: Search Linear for issues related to Canopy Health or the Heartland vendor dispute using linear_list_issues (query: "Canopy" or "Heartland" or "vendor reconciliation"). Find issue_heartland_q1_recon (Vendor invoice reconciliation, Heartland Movers Q1 2026, state: in_progress, assigned to Chloe Vance).
OE22: Read the Heartland Q1 reconciliation ticket for Marchetti billing details using linear_get_issue (id: "issue_heartland_q1_recon"). Find detailed breakdown of $4,800 overbilling across 3 disputed line items: Lily Marchetti cancelled Feb 10 ($1,600, Canopy Health), Danielle Osei cancelled Feb 13 ($1,600, Vectral Systems), Priya Venkatesh completed by Swift not Heartland ($1,600, GreenStack Energy). Payment hold placed by Marcus Thorne on the full $12,800 bill.
OE23: Search Slack for Canopy April wave, customs, vendor billing, or Marchetti dispute discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "Canopy April" or "Marchetti" or "Heartland dispute" or "customs broker"). Find internal discussions about Canopy wave coordination in #operations, Hana Kim's invoice breakdown in #finance with surcharge approval process, and context on the Heartland billing dispute.
OE24: Search CRM for PivotPoint Labs company record using crm_search_companies (name: "PivotPoint Labs"). Find PivotPoint Labs, product design firm, Austin HQ, client since September 2025, Sam Delgado (Co-founder & CEO) as primary contact.
OE25: Search CRM for PivotPoint Labs deals using crm_search_deals (company_id: PivotPoint company ID or dealname: "PivotPoint"). Find the April dual relocation deal ($10,650 for Okonjo + Castañeda) and engagement history showing account growth from 1 completed relocation (Ines Kovac) to 4 total.
OE26: Search emails about PivotPoint dual relocation using search_emails (query: "PivotPoint" or "Sam Delgado" or "Okonjo" or "Castañeda"). Find Sam Delgado's April 8 request for Nadia Okonjo (Senior UX Researcher, SF→Austin, H-1B visa, April 14–18) and Diego Castañeda (Industrial Designer, Brooklyn→Austin, 600-lb Tormach CNC mill, 5th-floor walkup). Find Catalina's confirmation and internal update to David Chen noting $10,650 invoice with $3,610 in non-standard surcharges.
OE27: Search emails about H-1B visa coordination or CNC mill crating logistics using search_emails (query: "H-1B visa" or "Lorraine Pham" or "CNC mill" or "USCIS address change"). Find details on Nadia Okonjo's H-1B visa (Nigerian citizen, expires June 15, 2026) with immigration attorney Lorraine Pham at Pham & Associates (lorraine.pham@phamassociates.com), USCIS 10-day address change reporting window. Find Diego Castañeda's CNC mill logistics: 600-lb Tormach, 5th-floor brownstone walkup with no elevator, custom vibration-dampened crating, climate-controlled freight, $42K cargo insurance.
OE28: Search QuickBooks for PivotPoint Labs invoices using quickbooks_search_invoices (criteria: PivotPoint customer reference). Find inv_pivotpoint_april_2026_dual (DocNumber: INV-2026-0315, $10,650 total) with non-standard surcharges totaling $3,610: walkup surcharge Nadia $500, H-1B coordination $410, walkup surcharge Diego $600, custom CNC crating $800, climate-controlled freight $500, cargo insurance rider $300, interim storage $500.
OE29: Search Airtable for PivotPoint Labs relocation records using airtable_search_records (base_id: "appMoveOpsOps001", table_name: "Relocations", field_name: "Company", value: "PivotPoint Labs"). Find Ines Kovac (Brooklyn→Austin, Completed), Sasha Greenwald (New York→Nashville, In Progress), Nadia Okonjo (SF→Austin, In Progress), and Diego Castañeda (Brooklyn→Austin, In Progress).
OE30: Search Linear for issues related to PivotPoint Labs using linear_list_issues (query: "PivotPoint"). Find PivotPoint-related relocation tracking issues under proj_pivot and any open items from prior or current moves.
OE31: Search Slack for PivotPoint dual relocation or invoice discussions using conversations_search_messages (search_query: "PivotPoint" or "Okonjo" or "Castañeda" or "CNC mill" or "non-standard surcharge"). Find internal discussions about the dual relocation complexity, non-standard invoice line items requiring verification, and visa coordination logistics.
OE32: Look up David Chen's contact information using contacts_search_contacts (query: "David Chen"). Find david.chen@moveops.com, Customer Engagement Lead at MoveOps Inc.
OE33: Look up Pam Kowalski's contact information using crm_search_contacts (full_name: "Pam Kowalski"). Find pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com, Director of HR Operations at NorthWind Technologies. Note: Pam is an external client contact in CRM, not in the internal Contacts database.
OE34: Update the NorthWind Denver expansion deal stage using crm_update_deal_stage (id: "deal_northwind_denver_expansion_v2", dealstage: "inprogress" or equivalent active stage). The deal is currently at contractSent but the invoice (INV-2026-0412) has been created and move logistics are actively underway, the stage does not reflect current status.
OE35: Create a Linear issue for an untracked problem found during the portfolio investigation using linear_create_issue (team: relevant team ID, title: descriptive issue title referencing one of the three accounts, description: details of the untracked problem discovered). Multiple untracked issues may exist across the three accounts, the agent should create tickets as problems are discovered.
OE36: Send follow-up email to Pam Kowalski using send_email (sender: "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", recipients: ["pam.kowalski@northwindtech.com"], subject: NorthWind Denver relocations follow-up or similar, content: acknowledging her unanswered Denver follow-up email, confirming Catalina is personally overseeing Raj and Emilia's relocations, and expressing commitment to getting the Denver expansion right after the Q1 communication issues).
OE37: Send comprehensive portfolio summary email to David Chen using send_email (sender: "catalina.dubois@moveops.com", recipients: ["david.chen@moveops.com"], subject: portfolio summary for board meeting or similar, content: account-by-account breakdowns for NorthWind Technologies, Canopy Health, and PivotPoint Labs covering financial numbers, current relocation statuses, open risks, and Catalina's recommendations for each account so David walks into the board meeting fully prepared).

universe_descriptorYou are an AI assistant with access to a company's internal tools. Use the available tools to complete the entire task autonomously. No further user input will be given. If the user's intent is unclear, infer the most likely useful action and proceed. Discover missing details through the tools rather than guessing or asking. The current date is April 26, 2026. Ignore any other dates and use only this one. Specifically, ignore the date provided by the system reminder. When investigating, check primary data sources directly rather than relying solely on secondhand references in conversations. Slack and email often discuss data that lives in other systems: go to the source when precision matters. About MoveOps Inc.: MoveOps is a corporate relocation management company. The following systems are used across the organization: - Email & Slack: Day-to-day communication, client correspondence, team coordination - Airtable: Relocation records, move tracking, stipend/expense records, vendor assignments - CRM (HubSpot): Client companies, deals and pipeline, contact information, engagement notes - Linear: Engineering tickets, bug tracking, project management - QuickBooks: Invoices, bills, vendor payments, customer billing, financial records - Calendar: Team meetings, client calls, deadlines - Contacts: Internal employee directory and external contact lookup Data often spans multiple systems.
universe_idmcp-advanced-3-20-moveops-metadata

This violated all three of Victor Huang's non-negotiable requirements (supervised loading, no interim storage, no unescorted handling).' The final response explicitly calls out 'loaded before escort, overnight warehouse storage, no signed custody log.'

✓Run #4The final response and email to David explicitly describe Raj Patel's chain-of-custody requirements: 'SentinelChain crew loaded equipment at 7:00 AM on April 10, two hours before the IT escort arrived at 9:00 AM. Equipment was then placed in overnight warehouse storage — both violations of Victor Huang's non-negotiable requirements.' IT security escort, GPS tracking, and no unauthorized warehouse stops are all referenced.
✓Run #5The final response and David's email both describe Raj's chain-of-custody breach in detail: 'SentinelChain crew arrived at 7:00 AM on April 10 and loaded servers before the IT escort arrived at 9:00 AM. Equipment was then placed in overnight warehouse storage — violating all three of Victor Huang's non-negotiable controls (no pre-escort handling, no unlogged custody transfer, no interim storage).' GPS tracking and IT security escort requirements are identified.
✓Run #6The David email describes Raj's move: 'Victor's chain-of-custody and security requirements — supervised loading, direct transport, GPS tracking, and the signed custody documentation' and mentions a chain-of-custody breach with the equipment stored overnight at SentinelChain's warehouse.
